intro: Viorna bakeri, also known as Wild Potato Vine, is a perennial climbing plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for soothing skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Viorna bakeri include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Viorna bakeri is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
